summary: Bonamy and Bousquet asked whether bounding the clique-tree degree D of a chordal graph restores polynomial tractability for deciding connectivity of TS_k(G). Adak, Nanoti, and Tale (arXiv:2502.12749, 2025) directly and negatively answer this: TS-Connectivity is co-NP-hard on chordal graphs of maximum clique-tree degree 4, and TS-Reachability is NP-hard already at degree 3. Together these results show that bounded clique-tree degree does not make the problem polynomial-time tractable.

since_posted.title: Revisiting Token Sliding on Chordal Graphs
since_posted.authors: Rajat Adak, Saraswati Girish Nanoti, Prafullkumar Tale
since_posted.claim: Proves TS-Connectivity is co-NP-hard on chordal graphs of maximum clique-tree degree 4 (Theorem 1.1), directly and negatively answering Question 1 of arXiv:1605.00442.

search_queries: token sliding chordal graphs clique-tree degree polynomial complexity Bonamy Bousquet 1605.00442
token sliding reconfiguration chordal graphs connectivity NP-hard polynomial 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021
notes: The question asked whether fixing any constant D makes TS_k(G) connectivity polynomial on chordal graphs of clique-tree degree at most D. The 2025 paper arXiv:2502.12749 answers no: co-NP-hardness holds at D=4. The status d=2 and d=3 for TS-Connectivity specifically may remain open, but the question as posed (for 'any integers k, D') is answered negatively.
